preamble: The transition from a palatalized alveolar nasal [nʲ] to a true palatal nasal [ɲ]. This shift involves a change in the primary place of articulation from the alveolar ridge to the hard palate.

examples.note: Historical development of the palatal nasal from earlier palatalized sequences.

sources: Maiden, M. (1995). A Linguistic History of Italian.
Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
